message:
fix: add cross-process flock to recordBeadRespawn to prevent TOCTOU races (gt-gas0)

The respawn count state file (bead-respawn-counts.json) was protected by
an in-process sync.Mutex but had no cross-process locking. When multiple
witness instances ran concurrent patrols, they could read the same count,
increment, and write -- losing count updates. This delays spawn storm
detection past the DefaultMaxBeadRespawns threshold.

Add lock.FlockAcquire around the load-modify-save cycle in all three
functions that access the state file: RecordBeadRespawn,
ShouldBlockRespawn, and ResetBeadRespawnCount. The flock is non-fatal
(gracefully degrades if the lock file cannot be created) to preserve the
existing behavior of never blocking respawns on tracking failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
